generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) match the expression types.
Focus repairs on resolving unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL architecture to fix AGREE errors.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to fix type mismatches by altering AADL types.
